journal_entry.description: Purchase tea, beverages, and refreshments for employee break room
journal_entry.rationale: Tea and refreshments for employee break room are operating expenses (employee benefits/office supplies). Basic groceries including tea and beverages are HST-exempt in Canada, so the 10% tax stated is unusual but we follow the transaction text as source of truth. The tax paid is recorded as HST Receivable (recoverable input tax). Corporate credit card creates a liability (credit card payable).

output_debit_classifier.reason: Employee break room refreshments are an operating expense. The transaction states 10% sales tax (not the standard ON HST rate of 13%), but per system rules the transaction text is source of truth for stated tax rates. HST paid is recoverable (HST Receivable asset increases). Corporate credit card payment creates a liability. Debit tuple: 1 asset increase (HST Receivable) + 1 expense increase (refreshments) = [1,0,1,0,0,0]. Credit tuple: 1 liability increase (credit card payable) = [1,0,0,0,0,0].
